get_file_sizes.description: GET full file response (handle_request -> file_response streaming)
get_file_sizes.notes: Latency is dominated by handle_request setup (path parse, resolve, ETag, planner) not streaming. Streaming bodies are lazy; the response is returned before data is consumed.

head_file_sizes.notes: HEAD suppresses body via normalize_metadata; Content-Length preserved for payload-permitting status.

range_requests.notes: Range requests add ~14us overhead vs non-range (seek + range header parsing + Content-Range computation). Seek cost is uniform regardless of position.

conditional_requests.notes: 304 responses avoid file open and streaming entirely. ETag comparison is string-based.

error_paths.notes: 404 requires path parse + resolve. 403 dotfile rejects at parse level (very fast). 405 rejects at method check (fastest).

directory_index.notes: Similar to file GET: path resolve + index.html file open + streaming.

sequential_requests.description: Sequential requests on same ServeState (keep-alive proxy)
sequential_requests.notes: ~13.3us per request. No connection overhead measured (direct handler calls).

body_consumption.description: GET + full body read (collect streaming body into memory)
body_consumption.notes: Includes full streaming body consumption (chunk reads into Vec<u8>, Bytes conversion, collect). 128k takes ~22us per 8KiB chunk.

chunk_count_regimes.description: Streaming body with different chunk counts (DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE=8192)
chunk_count_regimes.notes: Per-chunk allocation cost scales linearly. 129 chunks: ~11.4us per chunk (includes Vec alloc + read + Bytes conversion + Frame wrapping).

directory_listing.description: Directory listing HTML generation at various entry counts
directory_listing.notes: Scaling is roughly linear: ~2.2us per entry for HTML generation + path resolution overhead. 1000 entries: ~2.2ms total.

head_vs_get.description: HEAD vs GET comparison for same file
head_vs_get.notes: HEAD saves ~1.1us by skipping streaming body creation. Difference is small because bodies are lazy.

known_limitations: Benchmarks measure handler latency only (no TCP, TLS, or HTTP framing overhead)
Warm page-cache only (files created immediately before benchmark)
macOS APFS only; Linux ext4/xfs and Windows NTFS may differ
Single-threaded tokio runtime in benchmark (production uses multi-thread)
No concurrent connections measured (sequential requests only)
Gnuplot not available; using plotters backend for criterion
variance: See individual p95_low/p95_high bounds. Outliers reported per benchmark.
